chunks.text: Welcome back aliens my name is Levin Reddy and let's continue with the series on Python.  So in the last video we have talked about loops right and specifically it was while  loop. In fact normally when we talk about while loop  it is for certain recreations and it is for certain conditions. Think  about list, think about tuple, think about string. So what I'm basically talking about

chunks.text: is let's say if you have a list here and in this list I have certain values. It can be  any random value let's say my name Naveen 65 and if I say let's say 2.5. It's very easy actually you can simply say  print and you can print the value of x. This will work. Okay let me run this code and you  can see we got the output. But what if you want to get the output individually? I want  to print the values one by one. So in this case you will be using a for loop. Now how

chunks.text: for loop works? So we have to say for then we have to use a variable we will say this  time i. Now i represents one element of this list at a time. So example initially I will  refer to Naveen then I will refer to 65 then I will refer to 2.5 one by one. So you have  to say for i in x. So one by one it will fetch the value and again this is a suit so you  have to use a colon there and you can write lines of statements. Again the point is you  need to make sure that you use a proper indentation. So I will be printing the value that's it.  I will be printing the value of i. That's it. There is no iteration unlike while here.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | For Loop in Python
chunks.text: So we don't initialize the variable. We don't take the condition and we don't increment  or decrement by ourselves. It will be done by for loop itself. This is pretty smart right?
